content: The omegon-demo project is a minimal Rust workspace used for demonstrating Omegon's TUI capabilities including the CIC instrument panel, memory system, and design tree.

Omegon uses a split-panel footer: left side shows engine config (model, tier, thinking, context gauge) and memory state (linked minds, fact counts). Right side shows four CIC instruments (context, tools, thinking, memory).

The CIC instrument panel uses four simultaneous fractal displays inspired by submarine Combat Information Center design. Each instrument monitors a different dimension: Perlin flow (context), Lissajous curves (tools), Plasma sine (thinking), CA waterfall (memory).
Color ramp follows CIE L* perceptual linearization: dark navy (idle) → teal (normal/brand center) → amber (hot/attention needed). Teal is the Alpharius brand color and represents nominal operation.
The demo project follows trunk-based development on main. All Omegon repos use main as the default branch, never master.

The memory waterfall instrument uses 1D cellular automata with CRT noise glyphs from the splash screen. Each mind gets its own column with independent CA state. Rule 204 (identity) at idle, chaotic rules (30/110/90/150) when active.
Context auto-compaction fires at ~70% utilization. The context instrument maps 70% fill to full amber. The operator sees full amber = compaction imminent.
Tool error state shows as amber Lissajous body with red border on the tools instrument. The red border is impossible to miss at any size. Error speed is deliberately slow (0.15) for an ominous feel.

The thinking instrument uses a quadratic speed ramp: color ramps up first (ignition), speed follows. At 30% thinking the speed is only 18% of max. This creates an 'ignition then engage' visual effect.

Ecological Interface Design (EID) principles from submarine CIC research guide the instrument panel: skill-based perception (gauges, colors), spatial consistency (fixed positions), stable layout (zeros shown), alarm states (color changes, not layout changes).
